demographics.occupation: manual/shift worker who wears heavy enclosed work boots for long back-to-back shifts

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The skin between my toes split wide open this morning. It's weeping, burning, and the pain is so sharp I can't even put my foot on the ground.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: One of the cracks between my toes suddenly tore open today and now it's raw, oozing and agony to stand on — I can't get a shoe on or walk properly.

symptom_narrative.onset: the deep split appeared this morning, but the itchy flaky skin between my toes has been there for a long time and has got worse over the last month
symptom_narrative.duration: long-standing itchy scaling between the toes; the painful open crack came on today
symptom_narrative.character: itchy, flaky, peeling skin between the toes that has gone soggy and white, and now a deep crack that is raw, red at the bottom, weeping clear fluid and smells bad; the soles are dry and flaky
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the crack is agony — a sharp pain when I put any weight on it, so bad I'm hobbling on my heel and can't wear a shoe
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: between my toes, mostly the outer two toes on my right foot, and dry flaky skin across the sole
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the itch and flaking have been building for weeks with all the sweating in my boots; the open crack and severe pain started this morning
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: keeping the weight off it and getting the boot and sock off to let it air
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: standing or walking on it, and being shut in sweaty work boots all shift
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: no fever that I know of, I don't feel shivery or unwell in myself, and I haven't noticed any redness spreading up my foot or leg
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had itchy peeling skin and a bit of a whiff between my toes on and off for a couple of years, but it's never split open or hurt like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can barely walk — I'm hobbling on my heel and I've had to keep my foot up

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: none — I don't take any regular medicines

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: I've been doing back-to-back long shifts in heavy enclosed work boots and my feet sweat a lot and stay damp all day

objective_data_offered.value: between the outer toes on my right foot the skin is white, thickened and soggy-looking, and there's a deep split about a centimetre long that is raw and red at the bottom, weeping a clear fluid and giving off a strong bad smell; across the whole sole the skin is dry, fine and flaky
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: described by the patient over video; not a clinician examination

objective_data_offered.value: sitting with my sore foot up, wincing whenever the toes are moved, and hobbling on my heel because I can't put the front of the foot down
I haven't measured my temperature but I don't feel hot, shivery or feverish
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient impression only; no thermometer reading taken

objective_data_offered.value: I can't see any redness or streaks travelling up the top of my foot or my leg, and I can't feel any lumps in my groin
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient self-inspection only; cannot exclude early deep infection by palpation
